# Tooltip

Displays brief contextual information when a control receives focus or is hovered.

## Installation

Add the Tooltip component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/tooltip
```

## Usage

Use Tooltip for a short label or explanation that supplements a control. It must not contain interactive content, and it must not be the only place to communicate essential instructions, validation, or the result of an action.

```vue
<template>
  <TooltipProvider>
    <Tooltip>
      <TooltipTrigger as-child>
        <Button variant="outline">
          Hover
        </Button>
      </TooltipTrigger>
      <TooltipContent>
        <p>Add to library</p>
      </TooltipContent>
    </Tooltip>
  </TooltipProvider>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Tooltip,
  TooltipContent,
  TooltipProvider,
  TooltipTrigger,
} from '@/Components/Tooltip'
</script>
```

## Provider and delays

Place one `TooltipProvider` around a group of tooltips or near the application root. The StackTrace provider defaults `delay-duration` to zero, so tooltips open immediately. Set a modest provider delay when a dense interface would otherwise show tooltips during incidental pointer movement; a Tooltip can override the delay for one trigger.

## Accessibility

The trigger must be reachable by keyboard so the same content appears on focus as on hover. Give icon-only buttons an `aria-label` even when they also have a tooltip: the tooltip supplements the accessible name rather than replacing it. A native disabled control cannot receive focus, so do not rely on its tooltip to explain why an action is unavailable; place that explanation in visible content or on an appropriate focusable wrapper.

Use `TooltipTrigger as-child` to preserve the original control. The child must forward merged attributes and events as described in the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition).

## State and positioning

Tooltips normally manage their own open state. Bind `v-model:open` only when another interaction genuinely needs to coordinate it. `TooltipContent` forwards positioning props such as `side`, `align`, and collision behavior, and defaults to a four-pixel side offset.

> **Use Hover Card for a rich preview: [Hover Card](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/hover-card)**
>
> Hover Card supports a larger supplementary preview behind a link. Tooltip should stay brief and non-interactive.

Hosted documentation: [Tooltip](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/tooltip)
